---
title: "Figure_2"
author: "Charlotte Uden"
date: "2026-01-23"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(scales)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(scales)
library(stringr)

```


Load configuration files
- location of model drivers, rds files (containing models), tables of parameter estimates and performance metrics, and figures. 
```{r}
read_properties <- function(file_path) {
  lines <- readLines(file_path)
  lines <- lines[grepl("=", lines)]  # only keep lines with key-value pairs
  key_vals <- strsplit(lines, "=")
  props <- setNames(
    trimws(sapply(key_vals, `[`, 2)),
    trimws(sapply(key_vals, `[`, 1))
  )
  return(props)
}

#config <- read_properties("/path/to/config/file/my_config.properties")  # path to your config file

# Extract values from config
drivers_root <- config[["drivers_root"]]
rds_root <- config[["rds_root"]]
table_root <- config[["table_root"]]
predictions_root <- config[["predictions_root"]]
figure_root <- config[["figure_root"]]

# Use the values to build full paths 
```

```{r}
df <- read.csv(file.path(table_root, "ignition_efficiency_model_performance.csv"))

# Define the desired order
desired_order <- c("climate", "fuel moisture", "vegetation", "climate & fuel moisture", "climate & vegetation", "fuel moisture & vegetation", "climate, fuel moisture & vegetation")

df <- df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    predictors    = factor(predictors, levels = desired_order),
    approach      = factor(approach, levels = c("glmnet", "Bayesian")),
    training_years = factor(training_years, levels = c("2001-2012", "2012-2018"))
  )
```



```{r}
# A base theme for a clean, publication-style look
base_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    #panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    #panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y       = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.box         = "vertical",
    legend.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9)
  )

# ------------------ AUC PANEL ------------------ #
p_auc <- ggplot(df,
                aes(x = AUC, y = predictors,
                    color = approach,
                    shape = training_years)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.8, alpha = 0.9,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  # --- confidence intervals ---
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = AUC_CI_low,
                     xmax = AUC_CI_high),
                 height = 0.25,
                 alpha = 0.6,
                 linewidth = 0.5,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0.4, 1.0),
    breaks = seq(0.4, 1.0, by = 0.1),
    labels = label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 25)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(
    x = "ROC AUC",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Approach",
    shape = "Training period"
  ) +
  ggtitle("(a)") +
  base_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
        plot.margin=ggplot2::margin(t = 10, r =10, b = 10, l = 30)) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# ------------------ PR AUC PANEL ------------------ #
p_pr_auc <- ggplot(df,
                aes(x = PR_AUC, y = predictors,
                    color = approach,
                    shape = training_years)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.8, alpha = 0.9,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  # --- confidence intervals ---
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = PR_AUC_CI_low,
                     xmax = PR_AUC_CI_high),
                 height = 0.25,
                 alpha = 0.6,
                 linewidth = 0.5,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0.2, 1.0),
    breaks = seq(0.2, 1.0, by = 0.1),
    labels = label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(
    x = "PR AUC",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Approach",
    shape = "Training period"
  ) +
  ggtitle("(b)") +
  base_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
        plot.margin=ggplot2::margin(t = 10, r =30, b = 10, l = 10)) +
  scale_y_discrete(position = "right",
                   labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 25)) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# ------------------ BRIER PANEL ------------------ #
p_brier <- ggplot(df,
                  aes(x = Brier, y = predictors,
                      color = approach,
                      shape = training_years)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.8, alpha = 0.9,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  # --- confidence intervals ---
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Brier_CI_low,
                     xmax = Brier_CI_high),
                 height = 0.25,
                 alpha = 0.6,
                 linewidth = 0.5,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  scale_x_reverse(
    breaks = seq(0.43, 0.13, by = -0.02),
    labels = label_number(accuracy = 0.01)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 25)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.43, 0.13)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(
    x = "Brier Score",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Approach",
    shape = "Training period"
  ) +
  ggtitle("(c)") +
  base_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
        plot.margin=ggplot2::margin(t = 10, r =10, b = 10, l = 30)) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# ------------------ CALIBRATION INTERCEPT ------------------ #
p_calib_intercept <- ggplot(df,
                  aes(x = calib_intercept, y = predictors,
                      color = approach,
                      shape = training_years)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.8, alpha = 0.9,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  # --- confidence intervals ---
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = calib_intercept_CI_low,
                     xmax = calib_intercept_CI_high),
                 height = 0.25,
                 alpha = 0.6,
                 linewidth = 0.5,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-2, 30, by = 1),
    labels = label_number(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") + #if intercept = 0, no bias
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2, 14)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(
    x = "Calibration Intercept",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Approach",
    shape = "Training period"
  ) +
  ggtitle("(d)") +
  base_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
        plot.margin=ggplot2::margin(t = 10, r =30, b = 10, l = 10)) +
  scale_y_discrete(position = "right",
                   labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 25)) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# ------------------ CALIBRATION SLOPE ------------------ #
p_calib_slope <- ggplot(df,
                  aes(x = calib_slope, y = predictors,
                      color = approach,
                      shape = training_years)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.8, alpha = 0.9,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  # --- confidence intervals ---
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = calib_slope_CI_low,
                     xmax = calib_slope_CI_high),
                 height = 0.25,
                 alpha = 0.6,
                 linewidth = 0.5,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-5, 111, by = 1),
    labels = label_number(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 25)) +
  #scale_y_discrete(labels = label_wrap(width = 40)) + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") + #if slope = 1, perfect calibration
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-4, 15)) + 
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(
    x = "Calibration Slope",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Approach",
    shape = "Training period"
  ) +
  ggtitle("(e)") +
  base_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
        plot.margin=ggplot2::margin(t = 10, r =10, b = 10, l = 30)) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# ------------------ COMBINE PANELS ------------------ #

# Extract the legend
#----
# Function to extract the legend from a ggplot object
extract_legend <- function(my_plot) {
  g <- ggplotGrob(my_plot)  # Convert plot to grob
  legend <- g$grobs[which(sapply(g$grobs, function(x) x$name) == "guide-box")][[1]]
  return(legend)  # Returns the legend as a grob
}

legend_plot <- ggplot(df,
                  aes(x = calib_slope, y = predictors,
                      color = approach,
                      shape = training_years)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.8, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(-4, 111),
    breaks = seq(-4, 111, by = 2),
    labels = label_number(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey70") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(
    x = "Calibration Slope",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Approach",
    shape = "Years"
  ) +
  ggtitle("(e)") +
  base_theme +
  theme(
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "cm"), # Add a small height back
    legend.key.width = unit(1.5, "cm"),  # Slightly reduced width
    legend.spacing.x = unit(0.5, "cm")
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(ncol = 3),
    shape = guide_legend(ncol = 3) 
  )

#----

legend_grob <- extract_legend(legend_plot)

blank_plot <- ggplot() + theme_void()

p <- grid.arrange(p_auc,p_pr_auc,p_brier,p_calib_intercept,p_calib_slope,blank_plot, legend_grob, nrow=4, ncol=2, heights=c(1,1,1,0.2), widths=c(1, 1), layout_matrix=rbind(c(1, 2), c(3, 4), c(5, 6), c(7,7)))

```


```{r}
ggsave(file.path(figure_root, "Figure_3.png"), plot = p, width = 10, height = 9, dpi = 1100, units = "in")
```
